text: LEBANON MOURNING: PM Nawaf Salam declares April 9 national day of mourning after 254 killed, 1,165 wounded in Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness. All public institutions closed, flags half-staff. Hospitals flooded with casualties.

sources.name: Xinhua — Lebanon declares mourning
The New Arab — Israel attacks kill 254

text: HEZBOLLAH ROCKETS: Hezbollah fires rockets at kibbutz Manara in northern Israel — first post-ceasefire attack. Hit open area, no injuries. Statement: 'Will continue until Israeli-American aggression ceases.'

sources.name: Rappler — Hezbollah rockets at northern Israel
Times of Israel — Rockets sound in northern towns

text: TRUMP WARNS: Posts military 'remains in place' around Iran. Warns 'the Shootin Starts, bigger, and better, and stronger than anyone has ever seen before' if ceasefire not complied with. 'There will be no enrichment of Uranium.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9
NBC News — Iran war live updates

text: OIL UPDATE: Brent rebounding to ~$97.39 after crashing to $96.24 close Apr 8. WTI at ~$94.41. AAA gas dips to $4.119/gal. Ceasefire uncertainty as Hormuz remains closed driving rebound. Markets: Dow +1,325 (+2.85%).

sources.name: OilPriceAPI — Live oil prices
AAA — Fuel Prices

text: IRAN MINES: IRGC publishes map showing 'danger zone' over Hormuz shipping lanes. Maham-3 and Maham-7 mines identified. Ships directed to alternative routes via Larak Island under IRGC coordination. 800+ vessels trapped in Gulf.

sources.name: JPost — IRGC navy issues map to guide ships around Hormuz mines
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

text: HORMUZ RE-CLOSED: Iran's IRGC says shipping stopped after Israel's Lebanon strikes. Press TV reports strait 'fully closed' — tankers turning back. White House calls closure 'completely unacceptable.' Only 2-3 ships transited since ceasefire.

sources.name: CNN — Hormuz traffic negligible
CBS News — Iran ceasefire live updates

text: HEZBOLLAH ESCALATES: 7+ rocket waves at northern Israel since midnight. 4 injured (3 Nahariya, 1 Kabri). Statement: 'Will continue until aggression ceases.' IDF reporting dozens of launches from Lebanon.

sources.name: Rappler — Hezbollah rockets
Times of Israel — Sirens northern Israel

text: LEBANON: Israeli strike on Abbassiyeh kills 7. Strikes continue across southern Lebanon — Kafra, Jmaijmeh, Majdal Selm. IDF Division 162 deploying to expand 'buffer zone.' Lebanon declared national day of mourning.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran war live April 9

text: OIL UPDATE: Brent $97.80 (+3.1%), WTI $97.53 (+3.1%) — rebounding on Hormuz re-closure fears. AAA gas $4.17/gal. Houthi missile targets Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted by USS Mason.

sources.name: CNBC — Oil prices Iran ceasefire
AAA — Gas Prices

text: LEBANON TOLL UPDATE: April 8 Operation Eternal Darkness death toll revised UP to 303 killed, 1,150 wounded (from initial 254). Total Lebanon war casualties: 1,888 killed, 6,092 wounded since March 2.

sources.name: CNN — Live updates April 9

text: DIPLOMACY: Netanyahu orders direct negotiations with Lebanon 'as soon as possible' — focus on Hezbollah disarmament. First meeting at US State Department next week. Decision came after Trump/Witkoff pressure to scale back Lebanon strikes.

sources.name: Axios — Netanyahu announces negotiations with Lebanon
Al Jazeera — Netanyahu ready for Lebanon talks

text: HORMUZ: 3 Chinese VLCCs (Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu) test Strait of Hormuz transit with Iraqi/Saudi crude. China FM confirms passage. Iraq says Iranian exemption applies. Mojtaba Khamenei vows 'new phase' for Hormuz management.

sources.name: Bloomberg — China tankers test Hormuz exit
Republic World — Mojtaba Khamenei new phase for Hormuz

text: HEZBOLLAH: ~70 rockets fired at northern Israel throughout Day 41 (Kiryat Shmona, Galilee, Taibe, Manara). One rocket hit home in Kiryat Shmona. Hezbollah claims response to ceasefire violations.

sources.name: Times of Israel — 70 rockets from Lebanon

text: OIL SURGES: Brent $98.26 (+4%), WTI $100.27 (+6%) — surging on ceasefire doubts, Hormuz still closed. Goldman flags $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month. Dow reversing yesterday's +1,325 rally.

sources.name: CNBC — Oil price jumps back above $100
Bloomberg — Goldman flags $100+ Brent

text: DRONE: IRGC shoots down Israeli Hermes 900 drone over Fars Province. Warns any airspace entry is ceasefire violation — 'decisive response will be delivered.'

sources.name: Times of Israel liveblog Apr 9

text: HOUTHI: Anti-ship missile fired at US-flagged Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden — intercepted by USS Mason. Houthis also claim targeting MSC Gina and MSC Darwin.

sources.name: Wikipedia — 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis

text: IDF: 98th Division captures nearly all of Bint Jbeil. IDF Chief Zamir visits troops: 'Iran is much weaker — Israel ready to return to combat at any moment.'

sources.name: Times of Israel liveblog Apr 9

text: HEZBOLLAH: Missile fired at Ashdod — deepest Hezbollah strike attempt in this war. Intercepted by Iron Dome. Sirens in Tel Aviv from falling fragments.

sources.name: Times of Israel liveblog Apr 9

text: IMF WARNING: Chief Georgieva says 'All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth.' Global forecast downgrade next week. Goldman puts US recession probability at 30%.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — IMF inflation warning

text: HEGSETH: Declares 'historic and overwhelming victory' for Operation Epic Fury. Says Iran 'begged for this ceasefire.' Urges Iran to surrender 970 lbs enriched uranium.

sources.name: NPR — Iran war updates

text: ISLAMABAD TALKS: VP Vance, Witkoff, Kushner heading to Pakistan for Saturday peace talks. Iran sending Ghalibaf + Araghchi. First in-person US-Iran negotiations since war began.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — US-Iran talks in Pakistan
Axios — Peace talks Pakistan

text: OIL: Brent $98.27 (+23.1%), WTI $97.53 (+29.2%) in early Apr 10 trading. Hormuz still effectively closed despite ceasefire. Markets cautious ahead of Islamabad talks.

sources.name: Trading Economics — Brent crude
